title: 10 best herbal teas for swollen feet
intro_desc: Herbal teas for swollen feet are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and roots, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. They help alleviate swelling in the feet by increasing urine production, thereby reducing fluid retention in the body. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for swollen feet include parsley, dandelion, hawthorn, and nettle tea. These teas can be consumed up to three times a day, depending on the severity of the swelling. By promoting healthy fluid balance, herbal teas can significantly improve the quality of life for individuals who suffer from swollen feet due to conditions such as edema, pregnancy, or prolonged periods of standing or sitting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower teas helps with swollen feet because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort by constricting blood vessels and promoting lymphatic drainage. As a result, the tea can help alleviate puffy, achy feet caused by prolonged standing or physical activity, allowing for improved circulation and reduced inflammation. This natural remedy is a comforting and effective way to soothe swollen feet and promote overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Arnica tea helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the feet due to its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory compounds, thereby alleviating discomfort and pain.

Antioxidant properties: Arnica's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic conditions like edema (swelling) in the feet.

Diuretic properties: Arnica tea acts as a natural diuretic, helping to increase urine production and eliminate excess fluids from the body, which can reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the arnica plant are rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with swollen feet.
Seeds: Arnica seeds contain a high concentration of volatile oils, particularly helenin, which helps to soothe and calm irritated skin and joints.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried arnica montana flowers in late summer when buds are fully open.
Rinse 2-3 teaspoons of dried flowers or 4-6 teaspoons of fresh flowers with pure water.
Combine the rinsed flowers with 250ml boiling water and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup or teapot.
Add honey to taste and serve warm or at room temperature within an hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Daisy teas helps with swollen feet because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal blend contains flavonoids, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by fluid retention. Additionally, daisy teas can promote healthy blood circulation, allowing for a more efficient removal of excess fluids from the body. This results in reduced puffiness and discomfort in the feet, leaving them feeling lighter and more comfortable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal daisy teas contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in swollen feet.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in herbal daisy teas help to protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate inflammation and swelling in the feet.
Diuretic properties: Herbal daisy teas have been shown to increase urine production, which can help reduce water retention and alleviate swelling in the feet by flushing out excess fluids and toxins.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the daisy plant are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with swollen feet.
Bark: The bark of the daisy plant contains flavonoids and terpenes that help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can alleviate symptoms of swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh daisy flowers at dawn or early morning when petals are fully open.
Rinse the collected flowers gently with cool filtered water to remove dirt and debris.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried daisy flowers or three tablespoons of fresh flowers.
Steep the herbs in one cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes depending on desired strength.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with swollen feet because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe and calm the affected areas. The tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, while its antifungal and antibacterial compounds prevent infection and promote healing. As a result, pot marigold teas provide relief from swelling, discomfort, and fatigue associated with swollen feet, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable and relaxed throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tea contains flavonoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in swollen feet.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, such as carotenoids and phenolic acids, help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to edema (swelling) in the feet.

Vasodilatory properties: Pot marigold tea has been shown to relax blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing fluid buildup in the tissues, which can help alleviate swelling and discomfort in the feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and soothe pain in the feet.
Bark: The bark has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making it beneficial for relieving swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers or purchase dried flowers in bulk.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried flowers or three to four teaspoons of fresh flowers per cup.
Heat filtered water to a simmer and pour it over the flowers in a heat-resistant teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for seven to ten minutes or longer according to desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola teas helps with swollen feet because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and inflammation. The herbs in gotu kola, such as centella asiatica, have been shown to improve circulation, which can help to reduce fluid buildup in the feet. Additionally, gotu kola has natural diuretic properties that aid in removing excess fluids from the body, further reducing swelling and discomfort. Drinking gotu kola tea regularly may provide relief for individuals who suffer from chronic foot edema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Gotu Kola's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, help reduce swelling and pain in the feet by inhibiting inflammation-causing enzymes.
Venotonic properties: Gotu Kola's venotonic effects help improve blood circulation, reducing fluid retention and edema in the feet, while also strengthening weakened veins and capillaries.

Antioxidant properties: Gotu Kola's antioxidant compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the skin and underlying tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to foot swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Gotu kola plant are used to make tea due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the feet.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Gotu kola contain compounds that have a soothing effect on swollen tissues, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Rinse one teaspoon of dried gotu kola herb or two teaspoons of fresh leaves with cold running water.
Combine the rinsed gotu kola herb or leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a tea infuser or cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the bioactive compounds to dissolve and release their flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and sediment.
Add one teaspoon of honey and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice if desired and stir well to combine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail teas helps with swollen feet because it is rich in silica, a mineral that strengthens connective tissues and improves circulation. As we age, our bodies lose collagen, leading to weakened blood vessels and poor circulation, which can cause fluid buildup and swelling in the feet. Horsetail's high silica content helps to restore collagen and improve blood flow, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy lymphatic drainage, ultimately relieving swollen feet and ankles.
remedy_properties: Silica content: Horsetail tea contains high levels of silica, which helps to strengthen connective tissues and improve circulation, reducing swelling in the feet.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in horsetail tea help to reduce inflammation and protect against damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to swelling and discomfort in the feet.
Diuretic properties: Horsetail tea has natural diuretic properties that help to increase urine production and flush out excess fluids, reducing swelling and water retention in the feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica and other minerals, roots are often used to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, helping to alleviate swelling in the feet.
Stems: Stems contain high amounts of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps to soothe and calm irritated skin and tissues, reducing swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried horsetail stems equisetum arvense for 10-15 minutes.
Rinse the harvested horsetail with filtered water and pat dry with paper towels for 5 minutes.
Combine 2 teaspoons of dried or 4 tablespoons of fresh horsetail with 1 quart boiling water in a teapot for 10 minutes.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes then strain into a cup using a cheesecloth or tea infuser for 5 minutes.
Add honey to taste if desired and enjoy your soothing herbal tea immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Meadowsweet teas helps with swollen feet because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The flavonoids present in meadowsweet tea have been shown to inhibit prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation in the body. By reducing these inflammatory compounds, meadowsweet tea may help alleviate symptoms of swollen feet such as pain, stiffness, and redness, providing relief from tired and achy feet.
remedy_properties: Salicylic acid: Meadowsweet tea contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in swollen feet.
Tannins: The tannins present in meadowsweet tea act as a natural astringent, helping to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling in the feet.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, found in meadowsweet tea have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm inflamed tissues, reducing redness and discomfort in swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, meadow sweet roots help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the feet.
Flowers: Rich in salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever, meadow sweet flowers soothe inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh meadowsweet leaves and flowers in the early morning or late evening for maximum potency.
Rinse the harvested plants with cold water to remove any dirt or debris then gently pat dry.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried meadowsweet herb or two teaspoons of fresh material per 8 ounce cup.
Heat filtered water to a simmer and steep the herb mixture for 5-7 minutes or until desired strength is achieved.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and enjoy warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with swollen feet because it contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate water retention. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice root, specifically glycyrrhizin, work to soothe swollen tissues and relieve pressure on the skin, allowing for improved blood flow and reduced swelling. Additionally, licorice tea's diuretic properties can also help to flush out excess fluids from the body, further contributing to its effectiveness in reducing foot swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and pain in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in licorice tea, including polyphenols and flavonoids, help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and swelling in the feet.
Potassium content: Licorice root is a rich source of potassium, an essential mineral that helps regulate fluid balance in the body. This can help reduce water retention and swelling in the feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used to make medicinal teas due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the feet.
Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making them effective for soothing swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots or dried root powder for optimal flavor.
Measure one teaspoon of dried licorice root or two teaspoons of fresh root for every eight ounces water.
Heat filtered water to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan and then reduce heat to simmer for five minutes.
Add the measured licorice root to the simmering water and steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids, serving warm or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel teas helps with swollen feet because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active ingredient, hamamelitanin, reduces swelling by constricting blood vessels and preventing excess fluid buildup. Additionally, witch hazel has astringent properties that help to reduce moisture in the skin, which can contribute to swelling. Drinking this tea regularly may also improve circulation, further reducing swelling and discomfort associated with swollen feet.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Witch hazel tea's astringent properties help to reduce inflammation and shrink swelling by constricting blood vessels, which in turn reduces fluid buildup in the feet.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in witch hazel tea help to combat oxidative stress and free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and swelling in the feet, promoting overall healing and recovery.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Witch hazel tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gallic acid, help to reduce pain, discomfort, and swelling associated with swollen feet by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing prostaglandins.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of witch hazel have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with swollen feet.
Bark: The bark of witch hazel contains tannins, which help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid buildup in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried witch hazel leaves hamamelis virginiana for optimal potency.
Combine one teaspoon of dried witch hazel leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves with boiling water.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes allowing the leaves to infuse their properties.
Strain the tea into a cup discarding the solids and adding honey or lemon if desired.
Enjoy the soothing and calming effects of the witch hazel tea within 15-20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy teas helps with swollen feet because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds like hedera helix, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. When consumed as a tea, English ivy's active ingredients are absorbed into the bloodstream, where they can target the root cause of inflammation in the body. This natural remedy helps to soothe and calm swollen feet, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with conditions like edema and poor circulation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, making it a natural remedy for relieving swollen feet.
Astringent properties: English Ivy's astringent properties can help constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention, which can contribute to swelling in the feet.
Antioxidant activity: The tea's antioxidant compounds help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate inflammation and swelling in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the feet.
Bark: Contains flavonoids that aid in reducing pain and discomfort associated with swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh english ivy leaves measuring one ounce for every cup of water.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with clean cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Steep the washed leaves in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the prepared tea in the refrigerator for up to 2 days before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort teas helps with swollen feet because its natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and soothe discomfort. The tea's antioxidants also work to promote blood circulation, which can help to alleviate fluid buildup in the feet by improving the body's ability to transport excess fluids away from the affected area. Additionally, st john's wort's antibacterial properties may help to prevent infections that can exacerbate swollen feet.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: St. John's Wort tea contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to swelling in the feet.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like hypericin and pseudohypericin, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain by reducing inflammation in the affected area.

Vasodilatory properties: St. John's Wort tea has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which means it can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, which may help reduce swelling in the feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen feet.
Leaves: Contain salicylic acid, similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce swelling and relieve discomfort in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers in morning dew.
Combine 2 teaspoons of dried st john's wort with 1 liter of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to release the herbs' medicinal properties slowly.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth carefully.
Drink the tea immediately, as it is best consumed within 30 minutes of preparation freshly.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat swollen feet with herbal teas is by using a combination of anti-inflammatory and diuretic herbs. Choose teas made from herbs such as dandelion, horsetail, or nettle, which have natural diuretic properties that help reduce fluid buildup in the body. Additionally, ginger, turmeric, or cinnamon teas can help reduce inflammation and soothe swelling. Steep the tea in hot water for 5-10 minutes, then let it cool before soaking your feet in it for 15-20 minutes, 2-3 times a day. Remember to stay hydrated and maintain a balanced diet to support overall health and reduce swelling. Always consult with a healthcare provider if swelling persists or worsens.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen feet that are treated with herbal teas include inflammation and water retention. For instance, ginger tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, making it an ideal remedy for swelling. Dandelion tea is another option, as it acts as a natural diuretic, helping the body eliminate excess fluid. Peppermint tea can also reduce inflammation, while chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedy, especially for those with underlying health conditions or those taking medication.
